text_blocks.text: The main coastal towns are, from the
west to the north-east, Minehead, Watchet,
and Portishead. The coastal area between
Minehead and the eastern extreme of the
administrative county's coastline at Brean Down is
known as Bridgwater Bay, and is a National
Nature Reserve. North of that, the coast forms
Weston Bay and Sand Bay whose northern tip,
Sand Point, marks the lower limit of the
Severn Estuary. In the mid and north of the
county the coastline is low as the level wetlands
of the levels meet the sea. In the west, the
coastline is high and dramatic where the plateau
of Exmoor meets the sea, with high cliffs and
